description: A multiplayer imposter-style game built with Next.js and Convex

instructions.for_agent: Only modify the 'passes' field of features. Do not remove, edit descriptions, or reorder features. Mark a feature as passes:true only after thorough end-to-end testing.
instructions.priority: Work on features in order from top to bottom. Complete one feature at a time.
instructions.testing: Verify each feature works as a user would experience it before marking as passing.

features.description: Waiting lobby has a shared counter with an emoji button that all players can click to increment
steps: Navigate to a game lobby as a player
Verify there is a button with an emoji (e.g. 🎉 or 👆) and a number displayed next to it
Click the button and verify the number increments
Open the same lobby in another browser/tab as a different player
Click the button from the second player and verify both players see the count update in real-time
Refresh the page and verify the counter persists with the correct value

features.description: Players table has an 'avatar' field that stores a random emoji assigned when they join a game
steps: Check convex/schema.ts to verify the players table has an 'avatar' field of type string
Join a game and verify the player record in the database has an avatar emoji assigned
Verify the avatar is one of a predefined set of fun emojis (e.g. 🦊🐸🦁🐼🦄🐙🦋🐢🦉🐝)

features.description: Messages table exists to store lobby chat messages with gameCode, playerId, playerName, avatar, message, and timestamp
steps: Check convex/schema.ts to verify a 'messages' table exists
Verify the table has fields: gameCode (string), playerId (string), playerName (string), avatar (string), message (string), sentAt (number)
Verify there is an index on gameCode for efficient querying

features.description: Backend mutation exists to send a chat message in the lobby
steps: Verify a 'sendMessage' mutation exists in convex/games.ts or convex/chat.ts
The mutation should accept gameCode, playerId, and message as arguments
The mutation should validate the player is in the game
The mutation should store the message with the player's name and avatar
The mutation should update the game's lastActivityAt timestamp

features.description: Backend query exists to fetch chat messages for a game lobby
steps: Verify a 'getMessages' query exists that accepts a gameCode
The query should return messages ordered by sentAt (oldest first)
The query should return playerName, avatar, message, and sentAt for each message
Test by calling the query and verifying it returns the expected format

features.description: Chat UI component displays messages with player avatar emoji, name, and message content
steps: Navigate to a game lobby
Verify there is a chat section visible in the waiting room
Verify each message displays: the player's emoji avatar, their name, and their message
Verify messages are visually distinguishable (e.g. own messages styled differently)
Verify the chat area scrolls when there are many messages

features.description: Chat input allows players to type and send messages
steps: Navigate to a game lobby
Verify there is a text input field for typing messages
Type a message and press Enter (or click send button) to send
Verify the message appears in the chat immediately after sending
Verify the input field clears after sending a message
Verify empty messages cannot be sent

features.description: Chat messages sync in real-time across all players in the lobby
steps: Open a game lobby in two different browser tabs as different players
Send a message from Player 1
Verify Player 2 sees the message appear in real-time without refreshing
Send a message from Player 2
Verify Player 1 sees that message appear in real-time
Verify both players see the correct avatar emoji and name for each message

features.description: Chat messages persist after page refresh
steps: Navigate to a game lobby and send a few chat messages
Refresh the page
Verify all previously sent messages are still visible
Verify the messages display with correct avatars and names after refresh
